A long-read, native-RNA alternative to the short-read inputs consumed by the wiki’s splicing-methods tools (rmats, leafcutter, suppa).

Sources

kim-2025-nanopore-direct-rna-sequencing-of — the paper that generated, validated, and analyzed the dataset.

wiki/entities/mprip.title: mprip
wiki/entities/mprip.content:
MPRIP (myosin-phosphatase Rho-interacting protein)
One-line: An rbfox2 splicing target whose exon-23-skipped isoform is oncogenic and pro-metastatic in pancreatic cancer.
In jbara-2023-rbfox2-modulates-a-metastatic-signature, RBFOX2 normally promotes inclusion of MPRIP exon 23; when RBFOX2 is depleted in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, the exon-23-skipped MPRIP isoform accumulates and contributes to enhanced migration and metastasis via RAC/CDC42 signaling (targetable with mbq167).
Referenced by

jbara-2023-rbfox2-modulates-a-metastatic-signature

wiki/entities/muscleblind.title: muscleblind
wiki/entities/muscleblind.content:
Muscleblind (MBNL protein family)
One-line: The MBNL family (MBNL1/2/3) of tissue-specific alternative-splicing regulators with position-dependent activity.
Muscleblind-like proteins bind YGCY/CUG-type motifs and act as position-dependent splicing regulators: binding upstream of a regulated exon tends to repress inclusion, while binding downstream tends to activate it. In han-2013-mbnl-proteins-repress-es-cell-specific-alternative the family (via mbnl1 and mbnl2) represses embryonic-stem-cell-specific exons. The family is also central to myotonic dystrophy pathogenesis.
Referenced by

han-2013-mbnl-proteins-repress-es-cell-specific-alternative

wiki/entities/music-assay.title: music-assay
wiki/entities/music-assay.content:
MUSIC (multinucleic acid interaction mapping in single cells)
One-line: A single-nucleus assay that jointly profiles multiplex DNA–DNA chromatin interactions, gene expression, and RNA–chromatin associations from the same nucleus.
What it is

Barcoding scheme with a per-nucleus cell barcode plus a per-complex complex barcode (10X GEM barcode + I7 index) that labels co-complexed molecules; distinct RNA vs DNA linkers separate RNA from DNA inserts.
Captures molecular complexes as clusters: DNA-only (DD), RNA–DNA (RD), and RNA–RNA (RR); clusters with ≥3 DNA reads record genuine multiplex (multiway) interactions, not just pairwise contacts.
Unlike ligation-based Hi-C/iMARGI (two inserts per read pair), each MUSIC read pair carries a single insert; read1 (28 bp) reads the 10X barcode/UMI.

Notable use

Applied to H1/E14 ES cells (2,546 H1 cells) and to 14 postmortem human frontal cortex samples (9,087 nuclei), where multiplex clusters recapitulated TADs and nested-TAD structure that pairwise contacts alone poorly reflected.
Developed by the Sheng Zhong lab (UCSD).

wiki/entities/mx-software.title: mx-software
wiki/entities/mx-software.content:
Mx (software)
Mx (Michael Neale) is the classic matrix-algebra structural-equation
modeling program for fitting biometric twin and family models — the canonical
specialized tool for ACE/ADE structural-equation-model analyses in behavior
genetics.
Role in this wiki
Mx is the incumbent that the biometrical-genetics topic’s sources seek to
make optional. Both rabe-hesketh-2008-biometrical-modeling-of-twin-and and
mcardle-2005-mixed-effects-variance-components-models cite Mx-style SEM
software (the “SEMA” family) as the traditional route, then show that the
equivalent variance-components-model can instead be fitted in standard
mixed-model tools (linear-mixed-model / generalized-linear-mixed-model)
such as gllamm and sas-proc-mixed — lowering the software barrier to
estimating heritability.

wiki/entities/ncbaf.title: ncbaf
wiki/entities/ncbaf.content:
ncBAF (non-canonical BAF / GBAF complex)
One-line: The non-canonical BAF (GBAF) SWI/SNF chromatin-remodelling complex, defined by BRD9 plus GLTSCR1/GLTSCR1L and targeting CTCF-associated loci.
Unlike canonical BAF and PBAF, the ncBAF complex is distinguished by its BRD9 bromodomain subunit and the GLTSCR1/GLTSCR1L scaffold, and it localizes to CTCF sites and promoters. In inoue-2019-spliceosomal-disruption-of-the-non-canonical, mutant SF3B1 mis-splices BRD9, inserting a primate-specific poison exon that triggers nonsense-mediated-decay and degrades BRD9 protein, thereby disrupting ncBAF and functioning as a tumour-suppressive mechanism that can be corrected with antisense oligonucleotides.
Referenced by

inoue-2019-spliceosomal-disruption-of-the-non-canonical

wiki/entities/neuro-diff-screen.title: neuro-diff-screen
wiki/entities/neuro-diff-screen.content:
Neuro_Diff_Screen (Neural Differentiation Screen web tool)
One-line: Interactive Shiny web tool released with amelan-2026-crispr-knockout-screens-reveal-genes for exploring gene essentiality and expression from a genome-wide CRISPR knockout screen of mouse neuronal differentiation.

URL: aa-shifman.shinyapps.io/Neuro_Diff_Screen/
Lets users query the screen results — neural differentiation essential genes (NEGs), growth-restricting genes (NGGs), and RNA-seq expression across differentiation days 0/4/10.
Intended to facilitate use of the dataset for NDD gene discovery and variant interpretation.

Connections

Companion resource to the pooled-crispr-screen described in amelan-2026-crispr-knockout-screens-reveal-genes.

wiki/entities/nrxn1.title: nrxn1
wiki/entities/nrxn1.content:
NRXN1 (Neurexin-1)
One-line: Presynaptic cell-adhesion molecule and synaptic organizer at chromosome 2p16.3, subject to extensive alternative splicing, whose heterozygous deletions cause neuropsychiatric disease.
NRXN1 encodes neurexin-1, a presynaptic transmembrane protein that organizes the synapse through interactions with postsynaptic partners. The gene produces distinct alpha- and beta-neurexin isoforms and undergoes extensive alternative-splicing at conserved splice sites (notably SS4/SS5) that tune its trans-synaptic binding specificity. Heterozygous deletions at the NRXN1 locus can produce either loss-of-function or gain-of-function splicing phenotypes and are recurrently associated with autism, schizophrenia, and other neuropsychiatric conditions. hiPSC-derived neuron models are used to dissect how these rare variants perturb NRXN1 splicing (see fernando-2025-phenotypic-complexities-of-rare-heterozygous).
Referenced by

fernando-2025-phenotypic-complexities-of-rare-heterozygous

wiki/entities/nucleotide-transformer.title: nucleotide-transformer
wiki/entities/nucleotide-transformer.content:
Nucleotide Transformer
One-line: A large DNA foundation model (0.5–2.5 B parameters) pretrained on thousands of genomes, evaluated as a general-purpose baseline for splicing and variant-effect prediction.
Nucleotide Transformer is a transformer-based genomic foundation model pretrained on 3,202 human genomes plus 850 additional species, spanning 0.5–2.5 billion parameters with 6-mer tokenization; training used 128 A100 GPUs over 28 days across 16 nodes. It illustrates the explosion of model scale relative to earlier task-specific tools like spliceai (~0.7 M parameters). Benchmarking finds that despite far greater scale and cost, such large foundation models do not guarantee better splicing prediction, and smaller task-specific models often remain preferable (see shen-2026-advances-and-challenges-of-splicing).
Referenced by

shen-2026-advances-and-challenges-of-splicing

wiki/entities/oncosplice.title: oncosplice
wiki/entities/oncosplice.content:
OncoSplice
OncoSplice is an unsupervised computational workflow that discovers cancer molecular subtypes and their candidate RNA-regulatory drivers from alternative-splicing profiles alone, introduced in venkatasubramanian-2025-splicing-regulatory-dynamics-for-precision (Venkatasubramanian et al., Sci. Transl. Med. 2025).
What it is

Builds on the authors’ nonnegative-matrix-factorization single-cell analysis lineage (ICGS) and operates on percent-spliced-in values computed by MultiPath-PSI in AltAnalyze.
Three coupled algorithms:

splice-ICGS — iterative clustering and guide-event selection for unsupervised patient splicing-subtype detection; iteratively defines then excludes confounding subtypes to surface rare discrete programs.
Bridger — supervised mutation/subtype prediction (matching subtypes to genetics and RBP-knockdown splicing profiles).
RBP-Finder — RBP regulatory prediction adapting the RELI transcription-factor inference framework, integrating CLIP-seq binding evidence, CiSBP-RNA motifs, and RBP differential expression.


Blinded to patient genetics until the final supervised step, so subtypes are defined by coordinated splicing dysregulation rather than prior genetic/expression labels.
In AML it recovered known splicing-factor/oncofusion subtypes, found >12 recurrent novel subtypes across cohorts, and defined the prognostic U2AF1-like and SRSF2-like programs (partial phenocopies of u2af1-S34 / SRSF2-P95 mutant splicing).
Open source: github.com/SalomonisLab/oncosplice-v1 (Zenodo doi:10.5281/zenodo.15133583); long-read companion AltAnalyze-LR at github.com/SalomonisLab/altanalyze3.

In this wiki

A subtype-discovery layer in the splicing-methods toolkit, distinct from per-event differential-splicing callers like rmats and leafcutter — it consumes percent-spliced-in values to cluster patients, not to call individual events.
Applied to cancer, making it a splicing-and-disease tool with a direct splicing-therapeutics readout (linking the U2AF1-like program to PRMT5/IRAK4).

Sources

venkatasubramanian-2025-splicing-regulatory-dynamics-for-precision — introduces OncoSplice (splice-ICGS + Bridger + RBP-Finder), applies it across Leucegene/TCGA/BEAT-AML/TARGET AML, and validates the U2AF1-like program with long-read scRNA-seq and PRMT5 inhibition.

wiki/entities/one-seq.title: one-seq
wiki/entities/one-seq.content:
ONE-seq
Library-based off-target nomination method for global-scale CRISPR editor specificity profiling that identifies population-specific, variant off-target effects (Petri et al., bioRxiv 2021).

Uses a synthesized oligo library representing candidate sites; a naked/cell-free nomination approach in the paper’s detection catalog (see stewart-2025-measurement-and-clinical-interpretation-of).
Its emphasis on population-specific/variant off-targets connects to variant-aware prediction (crisprme).

Related: genome-editing, noncoding-variants.

wiki/entities/onek1k-cohort.title: onek1k-cohort
wiki/entities/onek1k-cohort.content:
OneK1K Cohort
One-line: A population cohort of ~1,000 individuals of Northern European ancestry with matched genotyping and PBMC single-cell RNA-seq, used for single-cell eQTL/sQTL mapping across immune cell types.
OneK1K pairs genome-wide genotyping with single-cell RNA sequencing of peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) from roughly 980–1,000 donors. The matched design enables mapping of expression and splicing quantitative trait loci (splicing-qtl) at single-cell resolution, resolving how genetic variants shape splicing differently across distinct immune cell types. It serves as the basis for single-cell resolution studies of splicing regulation (see liang-2026-single-cell-resolution-of-splicing-regulation).
Referenced by

liang-2026-single-cell-resolution-of-splicing-regulation

wiki/entities/outrider.title: outrider
wiki/entities/outrider.content:
OUTRIDER
OUTRIDER (Outlier in RNA-Seq Finder) is an R/Bioconductor tool that detects
aberrant gene-expression outliers in RNA-seq for rare-disease diagnostics,
from the Gagneur lab — introduced in brechtmann-2018-outrider-a-statistical-method.
What it is

A denoising autoencoder controls gene covariation (technical/biological
confounders) to predict context-dependent count expectations; counts are modeled
as negative binomial with gene-specific dispersion, and outliers are
significant deviations → FDR-adjusted p-values.
Auto-tunes its encoding dimension by recall of in-silico corrupted counts (no
manual confounder correction). End-to-end package: gene filtering, aberrant-sample
flagging, aberrant-gene calls.

Standing

Beats PCA and PEER (prior log-normal covariation methods), especially for
low-expression and under-expressed outliers; recalled all 6 validated pathogenic
events in the Kremer rare-disease cohort; its outliers are enriched for rare
high-impact variants in GTEx.
~50–60 samples needed to recall complete-loss events.

In this wiki

The expression-outlier counterpart to aberrant-splicing detection (FRASER →
absplice2, same lab); a rare-disease RNA-seq diagnostic tool alongside the
splicing-methods toolkit. Relevant to rare-variant / splicing-and-disease
work and the brain QTL atlases (walker-2019-genetic-control-of-expression-and).

Sources

brechtmann-2018-outrider-a-statistical-method — the method paper

wiki/entities/pafa.title: pafa
wiki/entities/pafa.content:
PAFA (Prioritization And Functional Assessment)
One-line: A supervised algorithm and web platform that prioritizes and functionally assesses noncoding genetic variants associated with complex diseases by adding population-differentiation features and recalibrated training labels.
PAFA is a sparse L1-regularized logistic-regression classifier (built with LIBLINEAR) trained on 41,847 functional and 41,847 control variants curated from ClinVar, GWASdb v2, and the 1000 Genomes Project. It integrates three feature classes: evolutionary conservation (phastCons, phyloP; 46- and 100-way), genomic annotations from ENCODE (histone ChIP-seq, TFBS, open chromatin, RNA polymerase binding, TSS distance), and population differentiation measures — notably the fixation index FST and a dispersion score (DS) computed across five 1000 Genomes super-populations.
A distinguishing design choice is treating common 1000 Genomes variants more carefully than prior methods (which labeled them uniformly benign): PAFA uses low-FST variants ranked by a regression model, plus simulated rare benign variants, as controls. It also provides a gene-centric annotation layer and an online platform integrating 23 curated genetic/genomic resources (originally at http://159.226.67.237:8080/pafa).
Introduced by zhou-2018-prioritization-and-functional-assessment.

wiki/entities/pairadise.title: pairadise
wiki/entities/pairadise.content:
PAIRADISE
PAIRADISE (Paired Replicate Analysis of Allelic Differential Splicing Events) is a hierarchical statistical model and software program for detecting allele-specific alternative splicing (ASAS) from replicate or population-scale RNA-seq data. Introduced in demirdjian-2020-detecting-allele-specific-alternative-splicing.
What it is

Treats the two alleles of an individual as a matched pair and multiple individuals heterozygous for the same SNP as replicates, framing ASAS detection as paired-replicate differential alternative splicing.
Uses a binomial distribution to model inclusion/skipping read counts (capturing coverage-dependent percent-spliced-in uncertainty) and a logit-normal distribution for allele-specific inclusion variation across individuals; an additive parameter d_i is the interpretable allelic difference, tested by a likelihood ratio test (chi-square, 1 df) with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR.
Inputs: RNA-seq FASTQ and phased-genotype VCF files, plus reference genome, GTF annotation, and an RNA-editing mask. Aligns reads to haplotype-modified personal genomes (via the rPGA procedure, STAR) to reduce reference bias, then detects AS events with rmats.
Freely available as Xinglab GitHub software and a stand-alone Bioconductor R package; supports an equal-allele-variance user option (si1=si2) for more permissive detection.
Distinctive capabilities: aggregates ASAS signal across samples for higher power than per-individual tests, and detects rare-variant effects on splicing that conventional sQTL analysis filters out.

In this wiki

Relation to splicing-methods: a population-genetics method for allele-specific splicing, complementary to sQTL approaches.
Refines the rmats paired model, replacing its correlation-parameter covariance with an interpretable additive difference parameter; also uses rMATS for AS event detection.
Quantifies events with percent-spliced-in.

Sources

demirdjian-2020-detecting-allele-specific-alternative-splicing

wiki/entities/pangolin.title: pangolin
wiki/entities/pangolin.content:
Pangolin
One-line: A deep-learning splice-site/usage variant-effect predictor with a SpliceAI-like architecture, trained across four mammalian species, widely used as a splicing baseline.
Pangolin (Zeng & Li, 2022) combines an ensemble of SpliceAI-style submodels trained across four species and multiple tissues to predict splice-site usage and the effect of genetic variants on splicing. It is routinely used as a strong baseline in variant-effect benchmarks: newer models such as alphagenome report outperforming it on ClinVar splice-region, deep-intronic, and MPRA-tested splice-disrupting variants (see avsec-2026-advancing-regulatory-variant-effect-prediction), while empirical hexamer-based frameworks are compared against it for cross-species portability (see dent-2025-a-basic-framework-to-explain). It is also cited as the best substitution predictor in deep indel mutagenesis work (see baeza-centurion-2025-deep-indel-mutagenesis-reveals-the).
Referenced by

baeza-centurion-2025-deep-indel-mutagenesis-reveals-the
avsec-2026-advancing-regulatory-variant-effect-prediction
dent-2025-a-basic-framework-to-explain

wiki/entities/peds1.title: peds1
wiki/entities/peds1.content:
PEDS1 (plasmanylethanolamine desaturase 1)
One-line: Gene (MIM 610994; also known as Tmem189) encoding plasmanylethanolamine desaturase, the enzyme that introduces the characteristic vinyl ether double bond in the final step of plasmalogen biosynthesis; newly implicated as an autosomal-recessive neurodevelopmental disorder gene.

Identified as a neural differentiation essential gene (NEG) in the CRISPR screen of amelan-2026-crispr-knockout-screens-reveal-genes.
Human disease: bi-allelic frameshift variant chr20:g.50153533AG>A (c.104delC; p.Ala35Valfs*16) in two consanguineous families; affected individuals show microcephaly, global developmental delay, growth retardation, congenital cataracts, seizures — but lack the skeletal abnormalities of classic RCDP.
Functions in the plasmalogen (ether lipid) biosynthesis pathway alongside PEX7, GNPAT, AGPS, PEX5, FAR1; PEDS1 acts outside the peroxisome (in the ER).
Mouse Peds1 deficiency: accelerated cell-cycle exit, reduced progenitor proliferation, impaired neuronal differentiation and cortical migration; microcephaly (~14%/11% rostral/caudal brain-size reduction).
PEDS1-104delC is a loss-of-function allele: protein undetectable by western blot and loses ER (calnexin) colocalization in COS7 cells.

Connections

Central validated hit of amelan-2026-crispr-knockout-screens-reveal-genes; exemplifies the recessive-NDD-via-metabolism pathway class.
Relates to neurodevelopment, neurogenesis, and microcephaly.

wiki/entities/perturb-seq.title: perturb-seq
wiki/entities/perturb-seq.content:
Perturb-seq
Introduced in dixit-2016-perturb-seq-dissecting-molecular-circuits. Perturb-seq is a platform that couples a pooled CRISPR screen with a single-cell RNA-seq readout, so that each cell’s full transcriptome serves as the high-content phenotype of its genetic perturbation, paired with the MIMOSCA regularized-linear-model framework for decomposing those phenotypes.
What it is

Pooled CRISPR + scRNA-seq. Cells are infected with a lentiviral sgRNA library and profiled by droplet scRNA-seq in a single pool; the cell barcode (CBC) links each cell’s expression profile to its delivered perturbation.
Guide barcode (GBC) design. The lentiviral vector delivers an sgRNA (U6-driven) plus an expressed Pol-II transcript carrying a selection/fluorescent marker (Puromycin-T2A-BFP) and a barcode (GBC, an N18 sequence). Because the standard sgRNA is not polyadenylated and thus poorly captured by 3’/oligo-dT scRNA-seq, the expressed, polyadenylated GBC is what reports the sgRNA identity in the transcriptome; an enrichment PCR after whole-transcriptome amplification boosts GBC detection. GBCs are associated to their sgRNAs by sequencing the plasmid pool up front.
MOI tuning. By varying multiplicity of infection (MOI), the screen is set for single-gene effects (low MOI, ~one guide/cell) or for epistasis/genetic-interaction analysis (higher MOI, multiple GBCs/cell). GBC abundance per CBC is normalized to filter PCR chimeras/ambient contaminants and to retain genuine multi-guide cells.
MIMOSCA (Multi-Input-Multi-Output-Single-Cell-Analysis). A regularized (elastic-net) linear model that predicts each gene’s log-expression (matrix Y) from a design matrix X of guides plus covariates, fitting a coefficient/regulatory matrix β; significance by permutation test. It folds in technical covariates (transcripts/cell, cell quality), biological covariates (cell states/subtypes), an EM-like step that re-assigns which cells were effectively perturbed, and interaction terms for genetic interactions (additive / synergistic / buffering / dominant).
Compatible with KO and CRISPRi. Used here in the Cas9 knockout context; the companion study (Adamson et al., 2016) applies the same GBC/Perturb-seq design with CRISPRi.

In this wiki

A foundational functional-single-cell-genomics platform; the methods sibling Adamson et al. 2016 points to this same entity.
Its computational core is an instance of single-cell-perturbation-modeling and of model-interpretability (interpretable regulatory coefficients per guide/gene).
Conceptual lineage for later single-cell functional screens such as zhang-2026-uncovering-spatially-resolved-functional-genomics (spatially resolved CRISPR screening) and the single-cell regulatory-mapping work in chi-2026-single-cell-mapping-of-regulatory.

Sources

dixit-2016-perturb-seq-dissecting-molecular-circuits

wiki/entities/poder-annotation.title: poder-annotation
wiki/entities/poder-annotation.content:
PODER annotation
PODER (POpulation Diversity-Enhanced long-Read) is a cross-ancestry human gene annotation of 155,875 transcripts built from long-read RNA-seq of 43 lymphoblastoid cell lines spanning eight genetically-diverse human populations. Introduced in clavell-revelles-2025-long-read-transcriptomics-of-a-diverse.
What it is

A filtered set of 155,875 transcript models (each a unique intron chain), derived from 380,519 raw models and >800 M full-length ONT reads, classified by SQANTI structural category against GENCODE v47 (FSM/ISM/NIC/NNC).
73.5% already in GENCODE; 41,297 novel transcripts, of which 31,097 (75.3%) are absent from GTEx, ENCODE4, and CHESS3 catalogs. Includes 10,785 novel internal exons and 476 clustered novel intergenic genes.
Built by merging four discovery tools on intron-chain identity: FLAIR, IsoQuant, ESPRESSO (annotation-guided) and LyRic (annotation-agnostic).
Distributed alongside an “Enhanced GENCODE” (GENCODE + PODER novel transcripts), used to improve allele-specific transcript usage (ASTU) discovery, especially in non-European samples.

In this wiki

A reusable data-quality-and-bias resource: it makes the European bias in reference rna-seq annotations measurable at the level of alternative-splicing and population-specific splice junctions.
Supports allele-specific transcript-usage analysis relevant to splicing-qtl work.

Sources

clavell-revelles-2025-long-read-transcriptomics-of-a-diverse

wiki/entities/psi-sigma.title: psi-sigma
wiki/entities/psi-sigma.content:
PSI-Sigma
PSI-Sigma is a splicing-detection method that quantifies Percent Spliced-In (PSI) and differential splicing (DPSI) for individual alternative exons across all isoforms, handling complex multiple-exon-skipping (MES) and intron-retention (IR) events that two-isoform and isoform-resolution tools miss. Introduced in lin-2019-psi-sigma-a-comprehensive-splicing-detection-method.
What it is

Uses a modified PSI index (PSIΣ, the “Σ” denoting that all AS isoforms contribute to the PSI calculation), based on splice-junction reads of all isoforms between two constitutive exons.
A single alternative exon can carry multiple PSI values, one per isoform it appears in; estimates IR via intronic-read counts at the 1st/25th/50th/75th/99th intron percentiles.
Works on both short-read and nanopore long-read RNA-seq (the only one of the compared tools to support long reads).
Output: event coordinate, gene symbol, exon coordinate, event type, DPSI, p-value, adjusted P-value, and per-sample PSI. Open-source at github.com/wososa/PSI-Sigma; single-threaded.

In this wiki

Quantifies percent-spliced-in; catalogued under splicing-methods.
Benchmarked against and broadly comparable to rmats for single-exon events, but more comprehensive for MES/IR.

Sources

lin-2019-psi-sigma-a-comprehensive-splicing-detection-method

wiki/entities/psychencode-brainspan-deve.title: psychencode-brainspan-developmental-resource
wiki/entities/psychencode-brainspan-deve.content:
PsychENCODE / BrainSpan developmental brain resource
Introduced in li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis. A multimodal functional-genomic resource of the developing and adult human brain, generated by the NIH-funded PsychENCODE and BrainSpan Consortia, spanning the entire prenatal-to-adult lifespan.
What it is

A unified, largely same-donor multimodal dataset over 16 brain regions and the full developmental span (5 postconceptional weeks to 64 postnatal years) from 60 postmortem, histopathologically unremarkable human brains: bulk mRNA-seq and small-RNA-seq, single-cell and single-nucleus RNA-seq, DNA methylation (HumanMethylation450), histone-mark + CTCF ChIP-seq (H3K4me3, H3K27me3, H3K27ac, CTCF), and genotype.
Captures developmental dynamics of gene expression, alternative splicing (global percent-spliced-in trajectories; see percent-spliced-in), cellular composition, and epigenomic regulation, plus integration with neuropsychiatric GWAS via Hi-C and H3K27ac and gene coexpression modules.
Resource description and access: development.psychencode.org and www.brainspan.org. Part of the broader PsychENCODE Consortium (psychencode.org); the companion comprehensive resource paper (Wang et al., Science 2018) adds the consortium’s eQTL/isoform-QTL integration.

In this wiki

It is the primary reusable deliverable of li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis and the developmental backbone for the brain-splicing cluster, providing the prenatal-vs-postnatal splicing/expression context that brain-region splicing-qtl atlases (e.g. zhang-2020-regional-variation-of-splicing-qtls) build on, and a substrate for studying splicing-and-disease in neurotypical developmental context.

Sources

li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis

wiki/entities/psychencode2-developing-co.title: psychencode2-developing-cortex-lentimpra
wiki/entities/psychencode2-developing-co.content:
PsychENCODE2 Developing-Cortex lentiMPRA Catalog & Model
Introduced in deng-2024-massively-parallel-characterization-of-regulatory. A large-scale catalog of functionally tested gene regulatory elements and variants in the mid-gestation human cortex, together with sequence-to-activity deep learning models, generated as part of the PsychENCODE2 (“Decoding the Brain”) consortium.
What it is

A lentiMPRA dataset measuring enhancer activity for 102,767 candidate sequences in primary GW18 human cortical cells and 10-week cerebral organoids, including 46,802 active enhancer sequences and 164 differentially active variants (DAVs) altering allele-specific enhancer activity (cis-regulatory-elements).
Two paired libraries: a differentially accessible (DA) enhancer library derived from developing-cortex scATAC-seq peaks, and a variant library of 17,069 psychiatric-disorder-linked brain QTL allele pairs.
Accompanying sequence-to-activity deep learning models (a CNN + recurrent architecture per library/tissue) that predict lentiMPRA RNA/DNA activity from 270-bp sequence and, via in silico mutagenesis, predict the effect of untested nucleotide variants and classify TFs as activators vs repressors (cis-regulatory-grammar).
Source data are available through the PsychENCODE Knowledge Portal (Synapse, syn21392931).

In this wiki

The primary reusable deliverable of deng-2024-massively-parallel-characterization-of-regulatory and a human developing-brain massively-parallel-reporter-assay resource for functional-single-cell-genomics.
A reporter-assay counterpart to the developing-brain xQTL atlas psychencode-brainspan-developmental-resource and a sibling functional-variant resource to groover-2026-mapping-cis-regulatory-mutations-at and zhu-2026-integrative-analyses-elucidate-transcriptional-regulatory.

Sources

deng-2024-massively-parallel-characterization-of-regulatory

wiki/entities/ptbp1.title: ptbp1
wiki/entities/ptbp1.content:
PTBP1
What: The polypyrimidine-tract binding protein, an hnRNP-class splicing factor and tissue-specific repressor of splicing.
PTBP1 (PTB/hnRNPI) is a tissue-specific RNA-binding protein that, like other hnRNPs, mainly represses alternative-splicing by binding pyrimidine-rich splicing-regulatory-elements. As with hnrnpa1, its activator-versus-repressor effect is position-dependent, the hallmark of the splicing-code that distinguishes it from SR activators such as srsf1 lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing.
Role in splicing
PTBP1 (with hnRNPA1 and hnRNPA2) controls the PKM E9/E10 mutually exclusive exons, raising the PKM2:PKM1 ratio to enable the Warburg effect in cancer; the genes encoding these regulators are activated by MYC marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative. PTBP1 also links splicing to splicing-and-chromatin: the adapter MRG15 binds both PTB and the H3K36me3 histone mark, targeting PTB to transcripts such as FGFR2 exon IIIb lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative.
Disease / significance
PTBP1 is a determinant of splicing timing: it is among the proteins associated with post-transcriptional-splicing, where RNA loops mediated by PTBP1 shape regulation after transcription, complementing cotranscriptional-splicing rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing. Across species, a PTBP1 isoform reshapes splicing, tying it to organismal/evolutionary differences marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative. Its PKM control connects it to cancer metabolism (splicing-and-disease).
Sources

lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative — repressor; MRG15/H3K36me3 chromatin adapter; FGFR2
marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative — PKM/Warburg switch; cross-species isoform
rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing — post-transcriptional splicing timing; PTBP1 RNA loops

wiki/entities/ptbp2.title: ptbp2
wiki/entities/ptbp2.content:
PTBP2
One-line: Neuronal polypyrimidine-tract binding protein (paralog of PTBP1) that binds CU-rich intronic tracts and position-dependently regulates splicing of synaptic genes.
PTBP2 is a neuron-enriched RNA-binding splicing regulator and paralog of PTBP1. It recognizes CU-rich polypyrimidine tracts in introns and, in a position-dependent manner, activates or represses inclusion of target exons, shaping the splicing programs of synaptic genes. Among its targets, PTBP2 represses SYNGAP1 through an NMD-linked Exon 11 3′ splice site, coupling its regulation to nonsense-mediated-decay (see dawicki-mckenna-2023-mapping-ptbp2-binding-in-human).
Referenced by

dawicki-mckenna-2023-mapping-ptbp2-binding-in-human

wiki/entities/puffin.title: puffin
wiki/entities/puffin.content:
Puffin
What: An interpretable, deep-learning-inspired model of transcription initiation that predicts basepair-resolution initiation signal from promoter DNA sequence and distills a compact set of sequence rules explaining most human promoters. Introduced by Dudnyk, Shi & Zhou (2023).
Key facts

Two-layer design trained from scratch on sequence and initiation signal only (no known motifs supplied): a sequence-pattern activation layer and a transcription-initiation effect layer, both implemented as convolutions.
Learns three sequence pattern types: 10 motifs (51bp, effect range +/-300bp), 10 initiators (15bp, +/-7bp), and trinucleotides (3bp, +/-300bp); effects combine additively in log scale (multiplicatively in count scale) via a softplus output.
Discovers 10 reproducible motifs (>0.95 cross-replicate correlation) — direction-specific (TATA, YY1, U1 snRNP, Long Inr) and bidirectional (SP, NFY, ETS, ZNF143, NRF1, CREB) — each with a characterized position-specific effect curve.
Supports in silico TF knockout (zeroing a motif), motif contribution scoring, and basepair contribution scoring; validated against TF-depletion and motif edit experiments and against cross-mammal evolutionary conservation.
Companion prediction-focused model Puffin-D uses up to 100kb context (iterative upward/downward passes with residual connections) and predicts exact signal plus TSS/gene-level expression (>0.9 correlation), enabling virtual promoter-insertion screens.
Interactive web server: tss.zhoulab.io.

Appears in

dudnyk-2023-sequence-basis-transcription-initiation

wiki/entities/rbfox.title: rbfox
wiki/entities/rbfox.content:
RBFOX family
One-line: The Rbfox1/Rbfox2 family of sequence-specific splicing regulators that bind the (U)GCAUG motif to control alternative splicing in neuronal and developmental programs.
The RBFOX proteins (RBFOX1, RBFOX2, RBFOX3/NeuN) are RRM-domain RNA-binding splicing factors that recognize the (U)GCAUG element and regulate exon inclusion position-dependently. More than half of their functional binding sites lie in distal intronic regions and are evolutionarily conserved, indicating regulation from a distance. The RBFOX1 locus is genetically linked to autism, epilepsy, and FSHD (see lovci-2013-rbfox-proteins-regulate-alternative-mrna). See also the family member rbfox2.
Referenced by

lovci-2013-rbfox-proteins-regulate-alternative-mrna

wiki/entities/rbfox2.title: rbfox2
wiki/entities/rbfox2.content:
RBFOX2
One-line: An RRM-domain RNA-binding splicing factor recognizing (U)GCAUG that acts as a metastatic suppressor in pancreatic cancer and broadly regulates neuronal/developmental splicing.
RBFOX2 is a member of the rbfox family of splicing regulators, binding the (U)GCAUG motif to control alternative exon inclusion. In pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma it behaves as a metastatic suppressor: loss of RBFOX2 shifts splicing of targets such as MPRIP toward oncogenic, pro-metastatic isoforms (see jbara-2023-rbfox2-modulates-a-metastatic-signature). RBFOX2 also has a complex isoform structure in which low-abundance, N-terminally long isoforms are the functionally dominant splicing regulators despite their lower RNA level, as revealed by isoform-specific Cas13d knockdown (see schertzer-2025-cas13d-mediated-isoform-specific-rna-knockdown-with).
Referenced by

jbara-2023-rbfox2-modulates-a-metastatic-signature
schertzer-2025-cas13d-mediated-isoform-specific-rna-knockdown-with

wiki/entities/regvelo.title: regvelo
wiki/entities/regvelo.content:
RegVelo
RegVelo (regulatory velocity) is a Bayesian deep-generative framework (Theis / Sauka-Spengler labs) that jointly infers RNA velocity and a gene regulatory network, enabling regulon-wise in silico perturbation for single-cell-perturbation-modeling, introduced in wang-2026-regvelo-gene-regulatory-informed-dynamics.
What it is

Couples splicing kinetics with gene regulation: transcription rate is regulated and cell-specific, α_g = h([W·s(t) + b]_g), where W is a learnable GRN weight matrix. This makes the system a single coupled high-dimensional ODE solved with a parallel solver, instead of decoupled per-gene closed-form ODEs.
Built on veloVI; trained by stochastic variational inference; quantifies intrinsic/extrinsic velocity uncertainty. Prior GRN comes from multiome/ATAC-seq or databases as a learnable knowledge-graphs-style prior; sparsity via sparse prior network or Jacobian-L1 regularization.
Integrated with CellRank 2 to predict terminal states and simulate regulatory perturbations: masking a regulon and re-simulating dynamics yields nonlinear, long-horizon fate-probability shifts (not just first-order direction).

Standing

Outperforms scVelo, veloVI, TFvelo on velocity/latent-time and terminal-state recovery (TSI p < 0.001; hindbrain TSI = 0.95); driver-ranking AUROC = 0.95 in pancreas.
Beats dynamo and CellOracle on in vivo knockout-effect prediction: ~2× Spearman correlation with experimental MELD scores (0.52 vs < 0.25 single-TF) and precision/recall ≈ 0.6.
Discovered tfec (early driver) and elf1 (pro-pigment ETS factor) for zebrafish pigment-cell fate; validated by CRISPR-Cas9 + Perturb-seq and elf1 CUT&RUN.
Limits: linear regulatory interactions only; no direct motif/chromatin TF-activity yet; bootstrap uncertainty is computationally costly.

In this wiki

An artificial-intelligence / ai-in-genomics method; sibling in the single-cell-perturbation-modeling cluster to txpert and cmonge, but unique in perturbing the learned regulatory circuit and forward-simulating dynamics rather than predicting a static perturbed state.

Sources

wang-2026-regvelo-gene-regulatory-informed-dynamics — the method paper

wiki/entities/risdiplam.title: risdiplam
wiki/entities/risdiplam.content:
Risdiplam
One-line: FDA-approved oral small-molecule splicing modifier for spinal muscular atrophy that promotes SMN2 exon 7 inclusion.
Risdiplam stabilizes the U1 snrna–5′ splice-site A₋₁ bulge to include exons with weak 5′ splice sites, and prefers an N₋₃G₋₂A₋₁ motif at the 3′ exon end (contrast with branaplam, which prefers an “AGA” A₋₃G₋₂A₋₁ motif). Beyond SMN2, risdiplam acts genome-wide: it induces cryptic GA|GU 5′ splice sites, triggering nonsense-mediated-decay and downregulating genes such as MYB, illustrating a route to modulate otherwise “undruggable” targets (see lenkeit-2026-specificity-and-exon-target-space). Its exon target space is enumerable from splice-junction compendia via tools like snaptron.
Referenced by

lenkeit-2026-specificity-and-exon-target-space

wiki/entities/rmats-turbo.title: rmats-turbo
wiki/entities/rmats-turbo.content:
rMATS-turbo
rMATS-turbo is the fast, scalable re-implementation of rmats (rMATS v4.0.1 and above) that preserves the original statistical framework and command-line interface while adding a decoupled prep/post workflow, enabling alternative-splicing analysis on RNA-seq datasets with tens of thousands of samples. Documented as a protocol in wang-2024-rmats-turbo-an-efficient-and-flexible.
What it is

A two-step engine: a prep step turns each FASTQ/BAM file independently into a weighted splicing-graph file (.rmats; exons = nodes, junctions = weighted edges), and a post step integrates those files across samples to discover and quantify events. Prep can be parallelized/distributed; new samples only require re-running post.
Discovers and quantifies the five canonical event types — skipped exon, alternative 5′/3′ splice-sites, mutually exclusive exons, retained intron — reporting percent-spliced-in (PSI/Ψ) with junction-only (JC) and junction+exon (JCEC) read counts.
Retains the original rmats hierarchical statistical test for two-group differential splicing (with |Δψ| > c thresholding), and adds single-group mode, --statoff/--task stat decoupling, --novelSS unannotated splice-site discovery, --fixed-event-set, and --paired-stats (PAIRADISE).
Organism-agnostic, short-read only; companion rmats2sashimiplot renders events as sashimi plots. Limited to basic (binary) events.

In this wiki

The production successor to rmats and its methods paper shen-2014-rmats-robust-and-flexible-detection; the de facto reference event-caller in splicing-methods built on percent-spliced-in.
Widely used for cancer/EMT and sQTL splicing studies → splicing-and-disease.

Sources

wang-2024-rmats-turbo-an-efficient-and-flexible — the Nature Protocols protocol (workflow, features, two worked examples, benchmarks-by-citation)

wiki/entities/rmats.title: rmats
wiki/entities/rmats.content:
rMATS
rMATS (replicate Multivariate Analysis of Transcript Splicing) is the widely
used event-based tool for detecting differential alternative-splicing from
replicate RNA-seq data, from the Yi Xing lab (UCLA). It is the de facto
reference implementation of the ΔPSI event-caller class
named in splicing-methods.
What it is

A hierarchical statistical model + program that calls the five canonical AS
event types — skipped exon, alternative 5′/3′ splice-sites, mutually
exclusive exons, retained intron — and tests each for a between-group change in
exon inclusion level ψ (percent-spliced-in).
Distinctive features (shen-2014-rmats-robust-and-flexible-detection):
models both within- and between-replicate variation; supports paired
designs (exon-specific correlation ρ); and tests |Δψ| > c for a user-defined
threshold c rather than just inequality.

Lineage & ecosystem

MATS (Shen et al. 2012) → rMATS (2014, this entity) → later
rMATS-turbo (faster engine, widely used in practice).
Sits alongside other event/exon callers (MAJIQ, LeafCutter, DEXSeq) and
isoform-level methods (Cufflinks); benchmarked above the JSD-based Cufflinks and
DiffSplice in its paper.

Why it’s in this wiki

The empirical, short-read measurement counterpart to the broader toolkit in
sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying and the prediction line
(spliceai / splicing-code).
Heavily used for cancer/EMT splicing studies → splicing-and-disease.

Sources

shen-2014-rmats-robust-and-flexible-detection — the methods paper (model,
validation, benchmarks, design guidance)
sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying — places rMATS-style event
callers within the modern AS toolkit

wiki/entities/rnps1-ejc.title: rnps1-ejc
wiki/entities/rnps1-ejc.content:
RNPS1-EJC
One-line: An alternative exon-junction-complex flavor built from the EJC core plus UPF2, RNPS1, ACIN1 or PNN, and SAP18 (the ASAP/PSAP complexes).
The RNPS1-containing EJC is enriched at a subset of detained introns, drives UPF2-dependent nonsense-mediated-decay, and represses cryptic 5′ splice sites. It is one pole of EJC compositional modularity, contrasting with the casc3-ejc flavor (core + CASC3, promoting translation and AKT-mediated UPF2-independent NMD); the two flavors partition EJC targets and functions (see abshire-2025-gene-regulation-through-exon-junction).
Referenced by

abshire-2025-gene-regulation-through-exon-junction

wiki/entities/roulette-model.title: roulette-model
wiki/entities/roulette-model.content:
Roulette
Roulette is a genome-wide human germline mutation rate model at basepair resolution, implemented as per-pentamer, per-compartment logistic regression (91,176 models). It incorporates extended nucleotide context (six bases each side), strand asymmetry from transcription and replication direction, testis methylation and expression, and local rare-SNV density in 50 KB windows, trained on ~524 M rare noncoding SNVs from gnomAD v3.

Captures 80-90% of mutation-rate heterogeneity; outperforms the Karczewski/gnomAD and Carlson models on synonymous, noncoding, and de novo validation sets.
Uses a Poisson recurrence correction to convert monomorphic-site probabilities to the mutation-rate scale.
Released site-level estimates and covariate tables (genetics.bwh.harvard.edu/downloads/Vova/Roulette), usable as a neutral baseline for selection inference, demographic inference, and disease-gene mapping.

wiki/entities/rtca.title: rtca
wiki/entities/rtca.content:
RTCA
One-line: An RNA 3′-terminal phosphate cyclase newly validated as an endogenous exon-inclusion splicing regulator.
RTCA emerged as one of four “unexpected” hits from a tethered-function luciferase reporter screen of 718 human RNA-binding proteins. On knockdown it drives >500 differentially spliced skipped-exon events, and its eCLIP footprint centers on a GAAGA hexamer that behaves as an exonic splicing enhancer (see schmok-2024-large-scale-evaluation-of-the-ability). Whether it acts directly on splicing or via transcription-elongation/RNA-metabolism networks remains open.
Referenced by

schmok-2024-large-scale-evaluation-of-the-ability

wiki/entities/sage-cohort.title: sage-cohort
wiki/entities/sage-cohort.content:
SAGE cohort (Study of Autism Genetics Exploration)
A minimally ascertained clinical cohort of autism families recruited at the Seattle Children’s Autism Center. As sequenced in Guo et al. 2019 it comprises 180 families (578 individuals, 213 patients; both simplex and multiplex families, including cases with and without intellectual disability), selected only for a proband meeting ADOS/DSM-5 ASD criteria with DNA available from both parents. Analyzed by whole-genome sequencing (with exome and aCGH on subsets). Notable for lower ascertainment bias than the Simons Simplex Collection, enabling study of multiple-hit variant burden and phenotypic severity. Genome sequencing data available at dbGaP accession phs001740.v1.p1.

wiki/entities/sas-proc-mixed.title: sas-proc-mixed
wiki/entities/sas-proc-mixed.content:
SAS PROC MIXED
PROC MIXED is SAS’s general procedure for fitting linear-mixed-models —
fixed effects plus random effects with estimated variance components. Like
gllamm in Stata, it is a mainstream statistical tool, not a genetics-specific
one.
Role in this wiki
mcardle-2005-mixed-effects-variance-components-models uses PROC MIXED as the
representative of its MEMA (mixed-effects model algorithms) route: having shown
the biometric variance-components-model is algebraically equivalent to the
path-analysis structural-equation-model, it demonstrates the VCM can be
programmed in PROC MIXED — with practical advantages over SEM software
(mx-software) for missing data, covariates, and varying numbers of relatives
per family in a twin-and-family-study-design. (For binary outcomes the natural
extension is a generalized-linear-mixed-model, e.g. SAS PROC NLMIXED.)

wiki/entities/saser.title: saser
wiki/entities/saser.content:
saseR
saseR (Scalable Aberrant Splicing and Expression Retrieval) is a Bioconductor
R package from the Clement lab (statOmics, Ghent) that unifies differential
usage, aberrant splicing, and aberrant expression analysis of rna-seq in a
single negative-binomial framework — introduced in
segers-2026-saser-juggling-offsets-unlocks.
What it is

Built on the insight that replacing the library-size normalization offset
with the log of the total gene count turns standard NB DGE tools (edgeR,
DESeq2, DEXSeq) into estimators of feature usage — unlocking
differential transcript/exon usage and, via NB tail-probability scores, outlier
detection for rare disease (where no replicates exist).
A fast estimator (quadratic variance assumption → each Newton-Raphson step is
one matrix multiplication, all genes at once) scales to the many latent
confounders aberrant-expression cohorts need. RUVr removes confounders;
the Gavish–Donoho threshold picks the number of latent factors without
grid-search hyperparameter optimization.
Splicing workflows: saseR-bins (exon+intron bins), saseR-junctions, and
saseR-ASpli (annotation-free junction clusters).

Standing

Beats FRASER 2.0 (and LeafcutterMD, SPOT) for aberrant-splicing detection on
simulated Geuvadis data and ranks rare-disease genes better in the Kremer cohort —
partly because it uses bin reads, not junction reads only.
On par with OUTRIDER and OutSingle for aberrant expression but
much faster; matches DEXSeq/satuRn for differential transcript usage
while scaling far better.

In this wiki

The scalable, unified counterpart to the rare-disease RNA-seq diagnostic tools
here: aberrant expression (outrider) and aberrant splicing (FRASER, behind
absplice2) folded into one offset-driven framework, plus differential usage
(dexseq). Part of the splicing-methods toolkit on the
splicing-and-disease / clinical axis; design is future-proof to
long-read-rna-seq transcript counts.

Sources

segers-2026-saser-juggling-offsets-unlocks — the method paper

wiki/entities/sc-linker.title: sc-linker
wiki/entities/sc-linker.content:
sc-linker
sc-linker is an integrated framework, introduced in jagadeesh-2022-identifying-disease-critical-cell-types, for relating human diseases and complex traits to cell types and cellular processes by integrating scRNA-seq, epigenomic SNP-to-gene maps, and GWAS summary statistics.
What it is

A three-step pipeline:

Gene programs — from scRNA-seq, build continuous (0–1 scale) gene sets for cell types, disease-dependent states (disease vs healthy cells of the same type), and cellular processes (via non-negative matrix factorization / NMF, within and across cell types).
SNP annotations — link program genes to SNPs using two tissue-specific enhancer–gene strategies: Roadmap Enhancer-Gene Linking and the Activity-by-Contact (ABC) model.
Disease enrichment — apply stratified LD-score regression (S-LDSC) to the SNP annotations, conditional on the baseline-LD model, yielding a heritability enrichment score (E-score) with one-sided P values.


Released as open-source software with a web interface for visualizing enrichment results, plus postprocessed scRNA-seq data, gene programs, enhancer–gene annotations, and SNP annotations.
Code: gene-program construction / NMF at github.com/karthikj89/scgenetics; program × enhancer–gene processing at github.com/kkdey/GSSG. Uses S-LDSC (github.com/bulik/ldsc) and MAGMA v1.08 for benchmarking.

In this wiki

A functional-single-cell-genomics method that bridges single-cell expression and GWAS heritability, contrasted with gene-window gene-set methods (MAGMA, RSS-E, LDSC-SEG).
Applied to surface cell–disease links such as GABA-ergic neurons in MDD, microglia in MS/AD, and an M-cell program in ulcerative colitis.

Sources

jagadeesh-2022-identifying-disease-critical-cell-types

wiki/entities/scaf8.title: scaf8
wiki/entities/scaf8.content:
SCAF8
One-line: An SR-related CTD-associated factor newly validated as an endogenous splicing regulator of exon inclusion.
SCAF8 was one of four unexpected hits (with trnau1ap, rtca, and stau2) from a tethered-function screen of 718 RNA-binding proteins, validated by combining eCLIP, shRNA knockdown with RNA-seq, and affinity purification–mass spectrometry. Its eCLIP motif is poly-G (see schmok-2024-large-scale-evaluation-of-the-ability). Given its known role at the RNA polymerase II CTD, whether SCAF8 regulates splicing directly or via transcription elongation is an open question.
Referenced by

schmok-2024-large-scale-evaluation-of-the-ability

wiki/entities/scandel.title: scandel
wiki/entities/scandel.content:
ScanDel (scanning deletion)
Introduced in gasperini-2017-crispr-cas9-scanning-regulatory-elements. A multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 method that uses pairs of guide RNAs to program thousands of overlapping, kilobase-scale genomic deletions that densely tile a target locus, enabling functional scanning for regulatory elements in the endogenous genome.
What it is

Each cell receives a pair of gRNAs (delivered lentivirally at low MOI, <0.3), with each gRNA specifying one boundary of an intended deletion; NHEJ repair of the two Cas9 double-strand breaks excises the intervening DNA, yielding a programmed deletion much larger (1–2 kb) than the 1–10 bp indels of single-gRNA screens.
Paired 20 bp spacers are array-synthesized in cis and cloned into lentiGuide-Puro with two independent PolIII promoters (a U6-H1 design, which outperformed U6-U6 ~20% vs ~10% deletion rate); each gRNA expressed from its own promoter allows direct PCR/sequencing quantification of gRNA-pair abundance before vs after a functional selection.
High redundancy: overlapping tiling deletions cover each targeted base pair many times (median 27-fold in the HPRT1 demonstration), reducing noise and giving better coverage than PAM-limited individual-gRNA screens (which covered only ~70% of bases over the same region).
Controls are essential: self-pairs (each spacer paired with itself) flag effects of individual gRNAs, plus negative-control pairs. The programmed deletion occurs in only a minority of edited cells, so multiple independent pairs and robust selection are needed.
Demonstrated at the HPRT1 locus in HAP1 cells: 4,342 deletions tiling 206 kb, selected with 6-thioguanine for loss of HPRT function. Found no required distal regulatory element; apparent exon-proximal signal was an artifact of rare exon-extending deletions revealed only by long-read (PacBio) genotyping.
Reusable beyond this locus: compatible with any functional selection or expression-stratifying readout (e.g. GFP knock-in + FACS), and can be scaled by wider tiling or larger deletions. A method analogous to classic deletion-scanning but at high throughput in the native genome.

In this wiki

Sits in functional-single-cell-genomics as a genome-editing approach to functionally validating cis-regulatory-elements and assessing noncoding-variants in their endogenous context.
Complements episomal massively-parallel-reporter-assay approaches and the candidate-element/chromatin-accessibility framing used by reporter-assay studies such as deng-2024-massively-parallel-characterization-of-regulatory, groover-2026-mapping-cis-regulatory-mutations-at, and zhu-2026-integrative-analyses-elucidate-transcriptional-regulatory.

Sources

gasperini-2017-crispr-cas9-scanning-regulatory-elements

wiki/entities/scanfacex.title: scanfacex
wiki/entities/scanfacex.content:
ScanFaceX / ScanFaceN
ScanFaceX (Single-cell annotated Face eXpression dataset) is a single-cell transcriptome reference of the developing mammalian face: 57,598 mouse craniofacial cells across e11.5-e13.5, clustered into 42 primary clusters and annotated into 16 cell types (epithelial, mesenchymal, endothelial, neural-crest-derived including melanocytes, chondrocyte progenitors, osteoblasts, myocytes, sensory neurons, connective tissue).
ScanFaceN (Single-cell annotated Face eNhancer catalog) pairs the ScanFaceX cell-type annotations (via Seurat label transfer from snATAC-seq) with the human bulk-tissue enhancer set, yielding human craniofacial enhancer regions with predicted activity profiles across face cell types (115,521 accessibility-defined candidate regions; 10,038 differentially accessible across annotated cell types; 7,899 of 13,983 human enhancers overlap an accessible region).
Introduced by Rajderkar et al. (2023) as a community resource for craniofacial developmental and disease genetics; associated data also deposited in FaceBase and the VISTA Enhancer Browser.

wiki/entities/scarlink.title: scarlink
wiki/entities/scarlink.content:
SCARlink (single-cell ATAC + RNA linking)
Gene-level regulatory model for single-cell/single-nucleus multi-ome data (paired scATAC-seq + scRNA-seq). For each gene, SCARlink predicts single-cell expression from tile-level chromatin-accessibility using non-negative, regularized Poisson regression over 500 bp tiles spanning +-250 kb around the gene body, jointly modeling all regulatory tiles rather than pairwise peak-gene correlations, and without requiring peak calling.

Interpretation: regression coefficients and per-cell-type approximate Shapley values identify genomic tiles as putative cell-type-specific enhancers (cis-regulatory-elements), supporting model-interpretability.
Validation: predicted enhancer tiles enrich for promoter capture Hi-C interactions and for fine-mapped GWAS/eQTL variants; more disease-informative than ArchR peak2gene by S-LDSC.
Downstream: SCARlink-predicted expression vectors yield a chromatin-potential vector field for developmental trajectory inference.
Implementation: built in TensorFlow; integrates with ArchR-style tile preprocessing. Introduced in mitra-2024-single-cell-multi-ome-regression-models (Leslie & Dey labs, MSKCC).

wiki/entities/scgpt.title: scgpt
wiki/entities/scgpt.content:
scGPT
A generative pretrained transformer foundation model for single-cell multi-omics, pretrained on over 33 million human cells from CELLxGENE. scGPT jointly learns gene and cell embeddings using a specially designed attention mask for non-sequential omics data, then fine-tunes for downstream tasks: cell-type annotation, genetic perturbation prediction, multi-batch integration, multi-omic integration, and gene regulatory network inference. Introduced in cui-2024-scgpt-toward-building-a-foundation.

wiki/entities/sciso-portal.title: sciso-portal
wiki/entities/sciso-portal.content:
SCISO portal (developing-neocortex isoform atlas)
Online resource (sciso.gandallab.org/) for the long-read isoform-centric
transcriptome of the developing human neocortex, introduced in
patowary-2024-developmental-isoform-diversity-in-the (Gandal lab, PsychENCODE2).
What it is

Browsable atlas of 214,516 isoforms (72.6% novel vs Gencode v33) and >7,000 novel
exons from PacBio Iso-Seq of mid-gestation germinal-zone / cortical-plate tissue and
single cells.
Includes the custom developing-neocortex transcriptome annotation, isoform-switching
(GZ↔CP) calls, isoform co-expression/usage networks, and the isoform-resolved
reannotation of rare de novo NDD variants.

In this wiki

A developing-brain long-read-rna-seq isoform resource; the developmental
counterpart to the aged-cortex isoform atlas (brain-rna-isoform-browser-tool)
and a substrate for isoform-level disease work (bhattacharya-2023-isoform-level-transcriptome-wide-association-uncovers-genetic).

Sources

patowary-2024-developmental-isoform-diversity-in-the

wiki/entities/scisor-atac.title: scisor-atac
wiki/entities/scisor-atac.content:
ScISOr-ATAC
One-line: A single-nucleus method that jointly measures isoform-level splicing and chromatin accessibility from the same frozen brain nuclei.
ScISOr-ATAC pairs single-cell isoform RNA sequencing (Nanopore long reads over exon-enrichment-captured 10x cDNA) with snATAC-seq in the same nuclei, enabling cell-by-cell correlation of alternative-splicing and chromatin accessibility. Applied across cell states, brain regions, species, and Alzheimer’s disease, it shows that splicing and chromatin diverge and often vary in different modalities. It extends the ScISOr-Seq single-cell long-read isoform lineage (Tilgner group) by adding a chromatin readout, with cotranscriptional splicing as the mechanistic bridge (see hu-2026-combined-single-cell-profiling-of-chromatin).
Referenced by

hu-2026-combined-single-cell-profiling-of-chromatin

wiki/entities/scn2a-conditional-ki-mouse.title: scn2a-conditional-ki-mouse
wiki/entities/scn2a-conditional-ki-mouse.content:
Scn2a+/βKI conditional knock-in mouse
Used in tamura-2025-crispr-activation-for-scn2a-related. A conditional Scn2a knock-in mouse engineered so that one Scn2a allele can be switched from silenced (haploinsufficient) to functional on demand, to test whether restoring the second allele after development rescues SCN2A haploinsufficiency phenotypes.
What it is

Design. Exons 3–5 of one Scn2a allele are inverted, and an SA-2A-eGFP-polyA cassette is inserted upstream of the inverted exons; loxP and Lox2272 sites flank the elements. In the default state the allele is non-functional (mimicking haploinsufficiency), and eGFP marks the cell.
Cre-dependent reactivation. Cre recombinase excises the SA-2A-eGFP-polyA cassette and re-inverts exons 3–5 to their correct orientation, restoring a functional Scn2a allele. Injecting rAAV-EF1α-Cre-mCherry into the mPFC around P30 reactivated the allele in adolescence.
Made by Cyagen Biosciences via BAC-based targeting in C57BL/6 ESCs (confirmed by drug selection, PCR, Southern blot); germline transmission gave F1 heterozygous mutants.
Result. Adolescent Cre-mediated reactivation restored AP dV/dt, dendritic Ca2+ transients, and AMPA:NMDA ratio toward wild-type — establishing that late rescue of Scn2a is therapeutically meaningful.

In this wiki

A genetic-reversibility control model that motivates the scn2a-crispra rAAV therapeutic, isolating “does restoring the allele help” from “can CRISPRa deliver that restoration.”
A neurodevelopment disease-model reagent for SCN2A haploinsufficiency.

Sources

tamura-2025-crispr-activation-for-scn2a-related

wiki/entities/scn2a-crispra.title: scn2a-crispra
wiki/entities/scn2a-crispra.content:
Scn2a-CRISPRa (rAAV cis-regulation therapy)
Introduced as a therapeutic in tamura-2025-crispr-activation-for-scn2a-related. Scn2a-CRISPRa is a CRISPR-activation cis-regulation therapy (CRT) that upregulates the endogenous, still-functional SCN2A/Scn2a allele to correct haploinsufficiency, instead of delivering a replacement gene copy.
What it is

Two-component system. A nuclease-dead Staphylococcus aureus Cas9 fused to the VP64 transcriptional activator (sadCas9-VP64, ~3.3 kb, compact enough for a single rAAV) plus an sgRNA targeting the Scn2a promoter. dCas9-VP64 docks at the promoter and drives increased transcription of the endogenous allele.
Why CRT for SCN2A. SCN2A has a ~6 kb coding sequence, exceeding rAAV’s ~4.7 kb cargo limit, so classical gene replacement is impossible; CRISPRa sidesteps this by modulating the native gene and does not edit DNA (no double-strand breaks, no “DNA scars”).
Guide selection. Ten sgRNAs against mouse/human promoters were screened (Broad GPP tool) in Neuro-2a and SH-SY5Y; guides raising Scn2a mRNA ~2-fold (mouse) / ~1.3–1.5-fold (human) were AAV-DJ packaged and selected.
Delivery. Local stereotaxic (AAV-DJ) into mPFC, or systemic intravenous using the blood-brain-barrier-penetrant PHP.eB serotype for whole-brain expression; transgene expression persisted ~16 months.
Effect ceiling / safety. Raised Na_v1.2 only ~1.75× in WT neurons and did not increase network excitability or seizure burden — an apparent physiological ceiling on functional Na_v1.2 density (auxiliary subunits, scaffolds, post-translational modification).

In this wiki

A genome-editing-adjacent tool that activates rather than cuts DNA — the therapeutic use of a dCas9-VP64 activator delivered by rAAV.
Applied to neurodevelopmental / autism-associated SCN2A haploinsufficiency; rescues intrinsic and synaptic excitability in mouse and human neurons.
Uses the scn2a-conditional-ki-mouse and constitutive Scn2a+/− mice as test beds.

Sources

tamura-2025-crispr-activation-for-scn2a-related

wiki/entities/screen-ccre-registry.title: screen-ccre-registry
wiki/entities/screen-ccre-registry.content:
SCREEN / ENCODE registry of candidate cis-regulatory elements (cCREs)
What it is: The integrative annotation layer of ENCODE phase III: a genome-wide registry of candidate cis-regulatory-elements (cCREs) in human and mouse, plus SCREEN (Search Candidate cis-Regulatory Elements by ENCODE), a web server for querying and visualizing them.
Key facts

926,535 human cCREs (7.9% of GRCh38) and 339,815 mouse cCREs (3.4% of mm10), each requiring high DNase signal plus a high ChIP-seq signal (H3K4me3, H3K27ac, or CTCF).
Built by filtering/clustering DNase peaks into representative DHSs (rDHSs; 2.2M human, 1.2M mouse), keeping high-DNase rDHSs, then ranking against H3K4me3/H3K27ac/CTCF.
Classified by signal + genomic context into PLS (promoter-like), pELS/dELS (proximal/distal enhancer-like), DNase-H3K4me3, and CTCF-only groups; annotated by tiers (1a/1b/2) reflecting completeness of supporting data.
Served at screen.encodeproject.org; underlying data at the ENCODE Portal (www.encodeproject.org) and AWS Open Data.

Sources

moore-2020-expanded-encyclopaedias-of-dna-elements

wiki/entities/sensetoknow.title: sensetoknow
wiki/entities/sensetoknow.content:
SenseToKnow
One-line: A tablet app that plays strategically designed movies and a bubble-popping game while the frontal camera records a toddler, using computer vision and an XGBoost classifier over 23 digital behavioral phenotypes to screen for autism.
What it is

Administered on a tablet during a pediatric well-child visit; movies elicit autism-related behaviors (social attention, facial expressions, head movements, response to name, blink rate, motor behaviors) captured via the frontal camera.
Computer vision analysis (CVA) extracts 49 facial landmarks, head pose and gaze per frame; ML integrates the phenotypes into a combined diagnostic classification.
Outputs quality-of-administration and prediction-confidence scores plus SHAP-based individualized interpretability reports.

Notable facts

Validated in 475 toddlers (17–36 months); autism-vs-neurotypical AUC = 0.90, sensitivity 87.8%, specificity 80.8%, NPV 97.8%, PPV 40.6%.
Combining app output with the M-CHAT-R/F questionnaire raised AUC to 0.97.
Developed at Duke University (Sapiro and Dawson labs).

wiki/entities/sf3a3.title: sf3a3
wiki/entities/sf3a3.content:
SF3A3
One-line: A subunit of the U2 snRNP SF3a subcomplex whose acidic, phosphorylatable motif is a target of polyamine “metabolic shielding”.
SF3A3 carries an acidic (Glu/Asp-rich) region (~350–380) that cationic polyamines bind directly, physically occluding kinases such as casein-kinase-1/CK2 from phosphorylating it — the metabolic-shielding mechanism that couples polyamine metabolism to alternative splicing. NMR shows spermidine binds this region with Kd ≈ 0.7 mM and spermine ~3× tighter (Kd ≈ 0.22 mM); polyamines dose-dependently inhibit CK1α phosphorylation of recombinant SF3A3, and Sf3a3 serine knock-in mice (S365/S367/S369 → neutral) blunt the polyamine-dependent splicing response (see zabala-letona-2026-polyamine-dependent-metabolic-shielding-regulates-alternative).
Referenced by

zabala-letona-2026-polyamine-dependent-metabolic-shielding-regulates-alternative

wiki/entities/sf3b1.title: sf3b1
wiki/entities/sf3b1.content:
SF3B1
What: A core U2 snRNP / SF3b component that recognizes the intron branch point during 3’-end definition by the spliceosome.
SF3B1 is one of the splicing-factors that, together with SF1, the U2AF heterodimer (u2af1/U2AF2) and U2 snRNP, define the 3’ end of introns at splice-sites rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing.
Role in splicing
SF3B1 and PHF5A have a key role in branch-point (BP) recognition. A major rearrangement of SF3B1 from an open to a closed conformation is triggered by recognition of the pre-mRNA, with its HEAT-repeat domain contacting the BP adenosine, which is sandwiched between SF3B1 and PHF5A — a fidelity checkpoint confirming proper BP recognition rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing.
Disease & therapeutics
SF3B1 (also called SAP155) is recurrently mutated in myeloid malignancies and other cancers lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative. Mutations occur in 81% of MDS with ring sideroblasts and 15–36% of eye melanomas; K700E correlates with worse CLL prognosis but better MDS prognosis. Mutant SF3B1 activates cryptic 3’ss 10–30 nt upstream and converges on repressing the tumour suppressor BRD9 via a poison exon (splicing-and-disease). Small molecules (H3B-8800 in trials, pladienolide B, spliceostatin A) block the open→closed change (splicing-therapeutics) rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing.
Sources

rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing — BP recognition mechanism, mutation spectrum, inhibitors
lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative — SF3b/SAP155 in myeloid cancer; spliceostatin
marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative — convergence of myeloid splicing-factor mutations

wiki/entities/shank.title: shank
wiki/entities/shank.content:
SHANK
One-line: A postsynaptic scaffold protein (PDZ/SAM domains) that is a phase-separating component of the postsynaptic density.
SHANK organizes postsynaptic condensates as part of the GKAP–HOMER–PSD95–SHANK (GHPS) complex. In latrophilin-3 signaling, only the Gαs-coupled exon-31 LPHN3 variant carries a C-terminal PDZ-binding motif that binds SHANK and co-sediments with GHPS scaffold condensates, making SHANK-scaffold recruitment a splicing-controlled effector of synapse formation (see wang-2024-alternative-splicing-of-latrophilin-3-controls).
Referenced by

wang-2024-alternative-splicing-of-latrophilin-3-controls

wiki/entities/signature-tool.title: signature-tool
wiki/entities/signature-tool.content:
SIGnature
SIGnature is an open-source Python package (built on Captum) that scores
per-gene importance by computing gradient-based attributions over
single-cell-foundation-models, introduced in
gold-2026-scoring-gene-importance-by-interpreting. (Slug signature-tool
distinguishes it from the generic notion of a gene signature.)
What it is

Applies model-interpretability attribution methods (integrated gradients,
input×gradient, DeepLIFT) to pretrained scRNA-seq foundation models (SCimilarity,
scGPT, scVI, scTab, scFoundation) to measure each gene’s contribution to a cell’s
latent position.
Attributions are precomputed and queried as fast vector operations across
atlas-scale data (22M cells in minutes).

Standing

Beats expression-based signature scoring (highest F1 in 23/32 cell-type tests;
robust to depth/dropout); a query of the MS1 monocyte signature surfaced shared
activation in Kawasaki disease, SFTS and HLH, with in-vitro validation.
Attribution units are interpretive and inherit the underlying FM’s biases.

In this wiki

An artificial-intelligence / model-interpretability tool; complements the
perturbation-prediction siblings cmonge and txpert by interpreting
rather than predicting.

Sources

gold-2026-scoring-gene-importance-by-interpreting — the method paper

wiki/entities/simons-simplex-collection.title: simons-simplex-collection
wiki/entities/simons-simplex-collection.content:
Simons Simplex Collection (SSC)
One-line: A Simons Foundation cohort of ~2,600 simplex autism families (one affected child, unaffected parents and siblings) used for whole-exome and whole-genome de novo mutation studies.

Family-based design (trios and quartets) enables direct case (proband) vs. control (unaffected sibling) comparison of de novo mutation burden.
Foundational resource for establishing de novo LoF SNVs, CNVs, and indels as ASD risk factors.
Used by Dong et al. 2014 (787 families, 2,963 individuals) and many companion exome studies (Iossifov, O’Roak, Sanders, Neale).

wiki/entities/site-seq.title: site-seq
wiki/entities/site-seq.content:
SITE-Seq
Tagging-based biochemical off-target nomination assay for mapping the genomic landscape of CRISPR–Cas9 cleavage on cell-free genomic DNA (Cameron et al., Nat. Methods 2017).

A naked/cell-free nomination method in the paper’s detection catalog; sensitive but requires cellular verification of nominated sites (see stewart-2025-measurement-and-clinical-interpretation-of).

Related: genome-editing, circle-seq, guide-seq.

wiki/entities/slide.title: slide
wiki/entities/slide.content:
SLIDE (Significant Latent Factor Interaction Discovery and Exploration)
One-line: A first-in-class interpretable, data-distribution-free machine learning method that identifies significant standalone and interacting latent factors underlying outcomes of interest from high-dimensional multiomic datasets, with identifiability guarantees and false discovery rate control.
What it is
SLIDE is an interpretable latent-factor regression-based machine learning approach for high-dimensional, multicollinear omic data (single-cell, spatial, bulk and beyond). It makes no assumptions about the data-generating mechanism and provides biological inference beyond prediction. Introduced in Rahimikollu et al., Nature Methods 2024 (rahimikollu-2024-slide-significant-latent-factor-interaction).
How it works (three steps)

Latent factor discovery with identifiability — decomposes the data matrix X into an allocation matrix A and a latent factor matrix Z (X = AZ + E) using the previously described LOVE approach, giving a lower-dimensional representation (K < p). Unlike factor analysis or NMF, it comes with theoretical guarantees of unique identifiability, permits overlapping/correlated factors, needs no orthogonality assumption, and only requires anchoring each latent factor with two pure variables.
Significant standalone latent factor selection — a multistage adaptation of the knockoffs framework for FDR-controlled variable selection, applied to latent factors (not observed variables). Latent factors are split into sets, subjected to repeated rounds of knockoffs, and a stability parameter (‘spec’, selection frequency) is used to converge on stable significant standalone factors.
Significant interacting latent factors — pairwise interaction terms (Hadamard products Zi ⊙ Zj, each involving at least one standalone significant factor) are tested with knockoffs to extract significant nonlinear interactors.

Key properties
Eight distinguishing features: (1) identifiability of latent factors, (2) no assumptions on data-generating mechanisms, (3) applicability to any data modality, (4) handling of correlated factors, (5) handling of nonlinear relationships, (6) FDR control, (7) inference beyond prediction, (8) specificity for factors related to a chosen outcome.
Demonstrated uses

Systemic sclerosis (SSc) scRNA-seq: recovered nine significant latent factors explaining disease severity (Modified Rodnan Skin Score), recapitulated myeloid/fibroblast states and identified a novel keratinocyte-centric signature and HLA-signaling interaction; correctly honed in on the IL6/JAK/STAT mechanism of tofacitinib response.
Spatial partitioning of immune cells across 10X Visium, Slide-seq, MERFISH and CODEX modalities.
Spatial subclass separation of glutamatergic neurons in murine primary motor cortex.
Clonal expansion of CD4 T cells.

wiki/entities/smn2.title: smn2
wiki/entities/smn2.content:
SMN2
What: The human survival motor neuron 2 gene, a near-duplicate of SMN1 that fails to produce enough functional protein because of an alternative-splicing defect — the central target in spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) therapy.
Spinal muscular atrophy is caused by loss of SMN1; SMN2 persists but cannot compensate (rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing). The two genes are ~30 kbp duplicates differing in only 11 nucleotides, the key change being a C>T substitution in SMN2 exon 7 that increases its skipping (marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative). The predominant SMN2 mRNA therefore lacks exon 7 (E7) and yields a truncated, inactive SMN protein, while the minor E7-included variant is full-length and functional.
Both pre-mRNAs carry a splicing silencer in intron 7 bound by the repressive splicing-factors hnrnpa1 and hnRNPA2, which promote E7 skipping (marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative). This is a textbook case of splicing-and-disease driven by silencer-controlled exon definition (lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative).
Why it matters
SMN2 is the proof-of-concept target for splicing-therapeutics. Nusinersen (approved 2016), an antisense oligonucleotide, blocks the intron-7 silencer to displace hnrnpa1/hnRNPA2 and boost E7 inclusion (rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing, marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative). Risdiplam (FDA 2020), an oral pyrido-pyrimidinone small molecule, has similar effects by stabilizing U1 snRNA base-pairing at the SMN2 E7 5′ splice site (rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing). These were among the first splicing-corrective drugs.
Sources

marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative — 11-nt SMN1/SMN2 difference, C>T in E7, intron-7 hnRNPA1/A2 silencer, nusinersen/risdiplam.
rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing — SMA from SMN1 loss; nusinersen ISS-blocking ASO; risdiplam mechanism on U1–E7 pairing.
lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative — SMN2/SMA as a silencer-driven splicing disease and antisense target.

wiki/entities/snaptron.title: snaptron
wiki/entities/snaptron.content:
Snaptron
One-line: A database and query engine of splice junctions aggregated from large RNA-seq compendia (e.g. GTEx), used to enumerate and validate genome-wide splice sites and exons.
Snaptron lets researchers query junction support across massive sample collections. It is used to enumerate candidate splice sites/exons for splicing-modifier target-space analysis (see lenkeit-2026-specificity-and-exon-target-space) and to validate discovered junctions — for example, 94.3% of OneK1K single-cell junctions were documented in Snaptron in a single-cell sQTL study (see liang-2026-single-cell-resolution-of-splicing-regulation).
Referenced by

lenkeit-2026-specificity-and-exon-target-space
liang-2026-single-cell-resolution-of-splicing-regulation

wiki/entities/snmct-seq.title: snmct-seq
wiki/entities/snmct-seq.content:
snmCT-seq
snmCT-seq (single-nucleus methylcytosine and transcriptome sequencing) is a single-cell multi-omic method that jointly profiles DNA methylation (the methylome) and the transcriptome from the same individual nucleus. As applied in eyring-2026-single-cell-profiling-of-dna-methylation, it was developed by the Luo lab (Chongyuan Luo, UCLA) and is used there to dissect cell-type-resolved methylation in autism postmortem cortex.
What it is

Measures, per nucleus: cytosine methylation genome-wide (both CG and non-CG/CH contexts) plus paired RNA transcript levels — letting methylation findings be interpreted directly against expression in the same cell.
Modality separation trick: RNA-derived reads are engineered to carry high methylation levels, so genomic-DNA reads and RNA reads can be distinguished computationally by their non-CG (CH) methylation, rather than physically separated.
In the Eyring study, nuclei were FACS-sorted one-per-well into 384-well plates by NeuN (RBFOX3) status; the assay recovered ~66,000 QC-passing methylomes (median ~1,347/donor, ~1.5M reads each, ~83% genomic DNA) and paired RNA for a large subset.
Because methylation is highly cell-type specific, methylome data alone can annotate cell types (label transfer hold-out accuracy >=97.4% in this dataset), enabling inclusion of nuclei lacking high-quality RNA.

In this wiki

A single-cell method central to studying dna-methylation and epigenetics at cell-type resolution in the brain; its paired-modality design directly tests whether methylation changes track transcription (in ASD, they largely did not — see eyring-2026-single-cell-profiling-of-dna-methylation).
Distinct from chromatin-accessibility single-cell assays (e.g. single-cell ATAC-seq in kim-2024-leveraging-single-cell-atac-seq-and) — it reads the methylome rather than open chromatin, though both map cell-type-specific regulatory landscapes.

Sources

eyring-2026-single-cell-profiling-of-dna-methylation — applies snmCT-seq to 49-donor ASD/control prefrontal cortex (the method itself was previously developed by the same group).

wiki/entities/snu66.title: snu66
wiki/entities/snu66.content:
Snu66 (SART1)
One-line: Tri-snRNP spliceosome component (mammalian SART1) that carries the HIND element bound non-covalently by the ubiquitin-like protein hub1, enabling non-canonical 5′ splice-site usage.
Snu66 is a core component of the U4/U6.U5 tri-snRNP of the spliceosome. Its intrinsically disordered N-terminal region contains the conserved HIND (Hub1-interaction domain) motif — in yeast two HIND peptides — that hub1 (UBL5) binds through a ubiquitin-like surface distinct from SUMO–SIM, with high affinity (Kd ≈ 0.6 and 1.7 µM) (see mishra-2011-role-of-the-ubiquitin-like-protein). Hub1 binding remodels Snu66 while largely preserving core snRNP architecture, and is required for use of certain non-canonical 5′ splice sites and for alternative splicing (e.g. yeast SRC1). Δsnu66 mutants show broad splicing defects that worsen with additional Hub1 loss. Hub1 co-localizes with Snu66 in human nuclear speckles, and the mammalian orthologue is SART1.
Referenced by

mishra-2011-role-of-the-ubiquitin-like-protein

wiki/entities/spac-seq.title: spac-seq
wiki/entities/spac-seq.content:
SPAC-seq
SPAC-seq (spatial CRISPR screen sequencing) is a sequencing-based spatial
CRISPR screen platform that captures pooled sgRNA libraries alongside
single-cell-resolution spatial whole transcriptomes in situ, introduced in
zhang-2026-uncovering-spatially-resolved-functional-genomics.
What it is

A retroviral SPACseq plasmid embeds the sgRNA inside a GFP mRNA (Pol II) plus a
