complete-genome era (see olson-2023-variant-calling-benchmarking-era),
characterizing short tandem repeats (see
tanudisastro-2024-sequencing-characterizing-short-tandem-repeats), the
ChIP–seq assay (see park-2009-chip-seq-advantages-challenges), and the data
formats that stream large datasets to browsers (bigwig-bigbed, see
kent-2010-bigwig-bigbed-enabling-browsing).
Genome biology & translation. Horizontal gene transfer in eukaryotic genomes
(see keeling-2023-horizontal-gene-transfer-eukaryotes) and the delivery of
genomic medicine into national health systems (see
stark-2019-integrating-genomics-healthcare-global).

Sources (10)

ihgsc-2001-initial-sequencing-analysis-human — the public draft human genome.
nurk-2022-complete-sequence-of-a-human — the first complete (T2T-CHM13) human genome.
logsdon-2024-variation-and-evolution-of-complete — variation & evolution of complete human centromeres.
liao-2023-draft-human-pangenome-reference — the HPRC draft pangenome reference.
olson-2023-variant-calling-benchmarking-era — variant calling & benchmarking with complete references.
tanudisastro-2024-sequencing-characterizing-short-tandem-repeats — sequencing short tandem repeats.
park-2009-chip-seq-advantages-challenges — ChIP–seq, advantages and challenges.
kent-2010-bigwig-bigbed-enabling-browsing — BigWig/BigBed data formats.
keeling-2023-horizontal-gene-transfer-eukaryotes — HGT in eukaryotes.
stark-2019-integrating-genomics-healthcare-global — integrating genomics into healthcare.

Cross-topic

Feeds population-genetics and variant-effect work; the reference-genome shift
changes every downstream analysis.

wiki/topics/splicing.title: splicing
wiki/topics/splicing.content:
Splicing
Topic hub for pre-mRNA splicing and alternative splicing — the removal
of introns from precursor mRNA by the spliceosome and its regulation, which
together form a central layer of eukaryotic gene regulation. The wiki’s deepest
topic: this page curates the sources (grouped below), the concept map, and the
current synthesis.
Why it matters
Splicing converts intron-containing “mosaic” genes into mature mRNA via a
two-step transesterification reaction. alternative-splicing — the regulated
inclusion/exclusion of exons — lets one gene produce many isoforms, expanding
the proteome and underpinning organismal complexity, development, disease, and
therapy (see editorial-2025-rna-splicing-a-central-layer).
Sources (85)
Foundational biology & reviews

sibley-2016-lessons-from-non-canonical-splicing (2016) — non-canonical splicing: recursive, back-splicing/circRNA, exitrons, microexons, trans-splicing (NRG review)
szabo-2016-detecting-circular-rnas-bioinformatic (2016) — circular-rna detection from RNA-seq: artifacts & statistical controls (NRG review)
gebauer-2020-rna-binding-proteins-in-human-genetic (2020) — rna-binding-proteins in human genetic disease; ~3,470-protein RBP catalogue (NRG review)
giudice-2024-splicing-regulation-through-biomolecular-condensates (2024) — splicing regulation via biomolecular-condensates / LLPS / nuclear speckles (NRMCB review)
waldron-2016-splicing-linking-genetic-variation (2016) — splicing as a primary link from common variation to disease (NRG Research Highlight)
wrighton-2017-the-diagnostic-power-of-rna-seq (2017) — RNA-seq raises Mendelian diagnosis rate beyond WES/WGS (NRG Research Highlight)
sharp-1994-split-genes-and-rna-splicing (1994) — Sharp’s Nobel Lecture: discovery of split genes; spliceosome biochemistry
lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative (2015) — spliceosome as ribozyme; mechanisms of AS regulation (foundational)
nilsen-2010-expansion-of-the-eukaryotic-proteome (2010) — AS as the engine of proteome diversity; kinetic splice-site choice
wang-2008-alternative-isoform-regulation-in-human (2008) — landmark mRNA-seq survey; ~92–94% of genes AS; defines PSI (human)
pan-2008-deep-surveying-of-alternative-splicing (2008) — first deep mRNA-seq AS survey; ~95% of multiexon genes AS (human)
wang-2009-rna-seq-a-revolutionary-tool (2009) — framing review of RNA-seq for transcriptomics
kalsotra-2011-functional-consequences-of-developmentally-regulated (2011) — developmentally regulated AS networks
fu-2014-context-dependent-control-of-alternative-splicing (2014) — combinatorial, position-dependent RBP control of AS
witten-2011-understanding-splicing-regulation-through-rna (2011) — RNA splicing maps from CLIP + splicing data
wang-2008-splicing-regulation-from-a-parts (2008) — parts list of regulatory elements → integrated splicing code (ExonScan)
baralle-2017-alternative-splicing-as-a-regulator (2017) — AS in development and tissue identity
scotti-2015-rna-mis-splicing-in-disease (2015) — RNA mis-splicing in disease; ASO/small-molecule therapies
shine-2024-co-transcriptional-gene-regulation-in (2024) — co-transcriptional gene regulation (eukaryotes & prokaryotes)
marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative (2022) — physiology of AS across tissues, cancer, ASD
wright-2022-alternative-splicing-as-a-source (2022) — AS as an evolutionarily labile source of diversity
rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing (2022) — regulation + disease + first splicing therapies
choquet-2025-the-regulation-and-function-of (2025) — post-transcriptional-splicing as a regulatory layer
editorial-2025-rna-splicing-a-central-layer (2025) — framing editorial
peynet-2026-emerging-role-of-g-quadruplexes (2026) — rna-structure-and-splicing (G-quadruplexes)

Methods & tools — quantification & differential splicing

mortazavi-2008-mapping-and-quantifying-mammalian (2008) — RPKM + ERANGE: first genome-scale RNA-seq
katz-2010-analysis-and-design-of-rna (2010) — MISO: Bayesian isoform quantification + design
trapnell-2010-transcript-assembly-and-quantification (2010) — Cufflinks: assembly + FPKM
wu-2011-splicetrap-a-method-to-quantify (2011) — SpliceTrap: Bayesian exon-inclusion
anders-2012-detecting-differential-usage-of-exons (2012) — DEXSeq: differential exon usage (NB GLM)
brechtmann-2018-outrider-a-statistical-method (2018) — OUTRIDER: aberrant expression-outlier detection (autoencoder + NB) for rare-disease RNA-seq
segers-2026-saser-juggling-offsets-unlocks (2026) — saser: swap the NB offset to unify differential usage + aberrant splicing/expression; beats FRASER, scales past OUTRIDER/DEXSeq (human, rare disease)
yano-2025-crosssplice-a-pipeline-for (2025) — CrosSplice: rare/deep-intronic splice-site-creating variants (SpliceAI + cross-tissue) as ASO targets
dawes-2022-splicevault-predicts-the-precise (2022) — splicevault: predicts which mis-splicing event a splice-site variant causes from 335K reference RNA-seq (300K-RNA Top-4); beats SpliceAI clinically (human)
shen-2014-rmats-robust-and-flexible-detection (2014) — rmats: replicate-aware ΔPSI event caller
alamancos-2015-leveraging-transcript-quantification-for-fast (2015) — SUPPA: PSI from transcript TPMs, ultrafast
bray-2016-near-optimal-probabilistic-rna-seq-quantification (2016) — kallisto: pseudoalignment quantification
li-2018-annotation-free-quantification-of-rna (2018) — LeafCutter: annotation-free intron excision; sQTL-scale
lin-2019-psi-sigma-a-comprehensive-splicing-detection-method (2019) — PSI-Sigma: all-isoform PSI; long reads
zhang-2019-deep-learning-augmented-rna-seq-analysis (2019) — DARTS: deep-learning-augmented differential AS
vaquero-garcia-2023-rna-splicing-analysis-using-heterogeneous (2023) — MAJIQ v2: LSVs across large/heterogeneous data
wang-2024-rmats-turbo-an-efficient-and-flexible (2024) — rmats-turbo: rMATS scaled to ×10⁴ samples
capitanchik-2024-from-computational-models-of-the (2024) — the splicing-code: PWMs → deep learning
sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying (2026) — the modern splicing-methods toolkit
conesa-2016-survey-of-best-practices (2016) — RNA-seq analysis best-practices survey
stark-2019-rna-sequencing-the-teenage-years (2019) — RNA-seq methods at maturity (long-read, single-cell, spatial)
hertel-2014-spliceosomal-pre-mrna-splicing-methods (2014) — Methods-in-Mol-Biol protocols volume

Population genetics & sQTL

park-2018-the-expanding-landscape-of-alternative (2018) — AS variation across human populations; splicing-qtl (human)
zhang-2020-regional-variation-of-splicing-qtls (2020) — regional sQTL structure in human brain (human)
demirdjian-2020-detecting-allele-specific-alternative-splicing (2020) — PAIRADISE: allele-specific AS (human)
mironov-2021-an-extended-catalogue-of-tandem (2021) — TASS catalogue: tandem splice sites (human, GTEx)
wang-2025-a-deep-dive-into-statistical (2025) — majiqtl: better sQTL statistics (MAJIQ representation + beta-binomial + weighted testing); explains neurodegenerative-disease GWAS, AD via MS4A3 (human)

RBP mechanism & maps

van-nostrand-2020-a-large-scale-binding-and-functional (2020) — ENCODE RBP map: 356 human RBPs (human)
begg-2020-concentration-dependent-splicing-is-enabled (2020) — Rbfox intermediate-affinity motifs; concentration-gated AS

Proteome

lau-2019-splice-junction-based-mapping-of (2019) — JCAST: splice-junction proteogenomics (human)

Long-read isoforms, single-cell & sQTL/disease (2024–2026)

kim-2025-nanopore-direct-rna-sequencing-of (2025) — direct RNA-seq: modifications × splicing × poly(A) on one molecule (molm13-drna-seq)
fu-2025-single-cell-and-spatial-alternative (2025) — longcell: single-cell/spatial AS from Nanopore long reads
quinones-valdez-2025-long-read-rna-seq-demarcates-cis (2025) — isolaser: cis- vs trans-directed splicing via long-read phasing (allele-specific-splicing)
clavell-revelles-2025-long-read-transcriptomics-of-a-diverse (2025) — poder-annotation: diverse-cohort long-read; ancestry bias in annotation (human)
bodelon-2026-native-long-read-rna-sequencing (2026) — native long-read: activation-induced isoform switching in human monocytes (human)
humphrey-2025-long-read-rna-sequencing-atlas (2025) — isomiga: human microglia isoform atlas + sQTL → AD/PD (human)
liu-2024-single-cell-rna-sequencing-of-peripheral (2024) — aida: 1M-PBMC multi-ancestry single-cell sQTL atlas → autoimmune disease (human)
tokolyi-2025-the-contribution-of-genetic-determinants (2025) — intervalrna-portal: blood e/sQTL → proteins, metabolites, disease (human)
venkatasubramanian-2025-splicing-regulatory-dynamics-for-precision (2025) — oncosplice: splicing-subtype discovery in leukemia; PRMT5/IRAK4 therapy (human)

Brain development, neuro & disease (19, 2011–2026)

patowary-2024-developmental-isoform-diversity-in-the (2024) — PsychENCODE2 long-read-rna-seq isoform atlas of developing neocortex (GZ/CP, single-cell); 214k isoforms, NDD variant reannotation (sciso-portal)
The splicing-in-human-brain cluster — see splicing-and-neurodevelopment; biology/disease context in brain-development-neurogenomics.
heberle-2024-mapping-medically-relevant-rna-isoform (2024) — long-read-rna-seq isoform atlas of aged human frontal cortex (AD); brain-rna-isoform-browser-tool
bhattacharya-2023-isoform-level-transcriptome-wide-association-uncovers-genetic (2023) — isotwas: isoform-level TWAS recovers ~60% more neuropsychiatric risk loci
bryois-2022-cell-type-specific-cis-eqtls-in-eight (2022) — cell-type-specific cis-eQTLs in 8 brain cell types; bulk dilutes ~46% cell-type-specific effects
ameur-2011-total-rna-sequencing-reveals (2011) — nascent transcription & widespread co-transcriptional splicing in human brain
mazin-2012-widespread-splicing-changes-in-human (2012) — lifespan splicing changes (dev → aging); NMD/poison-exons pattern
parikshak-2016-genome-wide-changes-in-lncrna (2016) — AS & lncRNA dysregulation in autism cortex
hwang-2016-dynamic-regulation-of-rna-editing (2016) — developmental A-to-I rna-editing atlas of human cortex
su-2018-alternative-splicing-in-neurogenesis-and (2018) — review: neuron-specific splicing regulators in neurogenesis
weyn-vanhentenryck-2018-precise-temporal-regulation-of-alternative (2018) — timed splicing modules in neural development (splicescope)
li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis (2018) — PsychENCODE/BrainSpan developmental brain atlas (psychencode-brainspan-developmental-resource)
walker-2019-genetic-control-of-expression-and (2019) — prenatal-brain e/sQTL atlas → SCZ/ASD (depict-viewer)
wen-2024-cross-ancestry-atlas-of-gene-isoform (2024) — cross-ancestry developmental-brain isoform/splicing-QTL atlas (devbrainhub)
nguyen-2025-aberrant-splicing-in-huntingtons-disease (2025) — HD aberrant splicing; TDP-43 + m6A
neil-2025-poison-exons-tuning-rna-splicing (2025) — review: poison-exons as a druggable handle (splicing-therapeutics)
wagner-2025-aberrant-splicing-prediction-during-human (2025) — absplice2: developmental-stage aberrant-splicing variant predictor
pigini-2026-poison-exon-splicing-in (2026) — brain poison-exons catalog + pathogenic variants (evonmd)
li-2026-the-contribution-of-short-tandem (2026) — splicestr: short-tandem-repeat → splicing variation in human cortex
michielsen-2026-decoding-exon-inclusion-in (2026) — neuron vs glia diverge from the splicing-code


Tags: source pages carry human_research and resource flags; population/RBP-map/proteome sources are typically human, and tool papers are resource:true.

Concept map

Machinery & chemistry — spliceosome · splicing · splice-sites
Regulation — splicing-regulatory-elements · splicing-factors · rna-binding-proteins · splicing-code · rna-structure-and-splicing · biomolecular-condensates
Timing & coupling — cotranscriptional-splicing · post-transcriptional-splicing · splicing-and-chromatin
Outcomes & event types — alternative-splicing · intron-retention · circular-rna · splicing-and-disease · splicing-therapeutics · splicing-and-evolution
Measurement & genetics — splicing-methods · percent-spliced-in · rna-seq · long-read-rna-seq · splicing-qtl · allele-specific-splicing
Brain & RNA-processing layers — splicing-and-neurodevelopment · poison-exons · rna-editing

Key entities

Factors — sf3b1 · u2af1 · srsf1 · hnrnpa1 · ptbp1
Gene examples — dscam · smn2
Structures — g-quadruplex
Tools — splicing-code / prediction — spliceai · darts
Tools — event/differential AS — rmats · rmats-turbo · miso · dexseq · splicetrap · psi-sigma · leafcutter · majiq-v2 · majiqtl · suppa · pairadise · exonscan
Tools — transcript quantification — cufflinks · kallisto · erange
Tools — long-read & single-cell AS — longcell · isolaser · oncosplice
Tools — variant-effect, clinical & neuro — absplice2 · splicevault · splicestr · splicescope · evonmd · outrider · saser · isotwas · crosssplice
Resources / datasets — encode-rbp-map · tass-catalogue · jcast · molm13-drna-seq · poder-annotation · aida · isomiga · intervalrna-portal · depict-viewer · psychencode-brainspan-developmental-resource · devbrainhub · brain-rna-isoform-browser-tool · sciso-portal

Syntheses

cotranscriptional-vs-post-transcriptional-splicing
alternative-splicing-functionality-debate
poison-exons-regulation-and-therapy

Current synthesis (across the 80 sources)

The brain is splicing’s deep end. A large cluster maps
[[splicing-and-neurodevelopment|splicing across brain development, aging and
disease]]: neuron-specific regulators (PTBP/Rbfox/Nova/SRRM4) drive timed exon
programs (su-2018-alternative-splicing-in-neurogenesis-and,
weyn-vanhentenryck-2018-precise-temporal-regulation-of-alternative); neurons
diverge from the splicing-code more than glia
(michielsen-2026-decoding-exon-inclusion-in); cross-ancestry developmental
e/sQTL atlases show isoform/splicing regulation mediates the most
neuropsychiatric GWAS heritability (wen-2024-cross-ancestry-atlas-of-gene-isoform,
walker-2019-genetic-control-of-expression-and,
li-2018-integrative-functional-genomic-analysis); and disease links span
autism (parikshak-2016-genome-wide-changes-in-lncrna), Huntington’s
(nguyen-2025-aberrant-splicing-in-huntingtons-disease) and druggable
poison-exons (pigini-2026-poison-exon-splicing-in,
neil-2025-poison-exons-tuning-rna-splicing). rna-editing is tracked as a
sibling RNA-processing layer.
Long reads are resolving isoforms directly. A 2024–2026 wave of
long-read-rna-seq moves splicing from event-inference to full-length isoform
observation — single-cell/spatial (longcell), cis/trans separation by phasing
(isolaser, allele-specific-splicing), cell-type isoform atlases powering
sQTL discovery (isomiga, aida), direct RNA-seq reading
modifications and splicing together (molm13-drna-seq), and exposing
ancestry bias in reference annotation (poder-annotation, a
data-quality-and-bias instance). Clinically, splicing programs define and
treat disease subtypes (oncosplice, leukemia).
The machine is settled; the regulation is the frontier. The spliceosome
is a ~4.8 MDa dynamic RNP of five snRNPs whose catalytic core is RNA — a
ribozyme related to group II introns (sharp-1994-split-genes-and-rna-splicing,
lee-2015-mechanisms-and-regulation-of-alternative); cryo-EM resolved most
states (rogalska-2022-regulation-of-pre-mrna-splicing). Open questions are
about control, not chemistry.
AS is nearly universal. Founding mRNA-seq surveys put AS at ~92–95% of
multiexon human genes (wang-2008-alternative-isoform-regulation-in-human,
pan-2008-deep-surveying-of-alternative-splicing); it expands the proteome
(nilsen-2010-expansion-of-the-eukaryotic-proteome) and is read out per-event
as PSI.
A position-dependent, combinatorial code. Weak metazoan splice-sites +
splicing-regulatory-elements read by splicing-factors/rna-binding-proteins
in a context-dependent way (fu-2014-context-dependent-control-of-alternative-splicing,
witten-2011-understanding-splicing-regulation-through-rna,
wang-2008-splicing-regulation-from-a-parts); RBP concentration itself gates
outcomes (begg-2020-concentration-dependent-splicing-is-enabled). Modeling
this splicing-code went from PWMs to deep learning (SpliceAI, darts,
capitanchik-2024-from-computational-models-of-the).
A mature measurement ecosystem. Splicing is quantified two ways —
isoform-based (cufflinks, kallisto, suppa) and event-based
(rmats/rmats-turbo, miso, dexseq, leafcutter, majiq-v2,
psi-sigma, splicetrap) — over a decade of rna-seq tooling
(sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying,
conesa-2016-survey-of-best-practices,
stark-2019-rna-sequencing-the-teenage-years). Tools disagree, so orthogonal
validation matters.
Genetics connects sequence to splicing to disease. Population RNA-seq maps
sQTLs and allele-specific splicing
(park-2018-the-expanding-landscape-of-alternative,
zhang-2020-regional-variation-of-splicing-qtls,
demirdjian-2020-detecting-allele-specific-alternative-splicing); RBP
binding/function atlases (van-nostrand-2020-a-large-scale-binding-and-functional)
and isoform/proteome catalogues
(mironov-2021-an-extended-catalogue-of-tandem,
lau-2019-splice-junction-based-mapping-of) ground the regulatory logic.
Timing is itself regulatory. Splicing is largely co-transcriptional, but a
large fraction is post-transcriptional
(choquet-2025-the-regulation-and-function-of,
shine-2024-co-transcriptional-gene-regulation-in), and intron-retention
is a widespread regulatory event.
Mechanism → medicine. Splicing-factor mutations drive cancer and genetic
disease; the first splicing therapies are approved (nusinersen, risdiplam,
eteplirsen) — see scotti-2015-rna-mis-splicing-in-disease,
splicing-therapeutics, splicing-and-disease.

Open questions

What triggers the co- vs post-transcriptional timing switch? (choquet-2025-the-regulation-and-function-of)
How much alternative splicing is functional vs noise? (marasco-2022-the-physiology-of-alternative / wright-2022-alternative-splicing-as-a-source)
Can splicing-code models become interpretable, tissue-specific, and predict activating variants? (capitanchik-2024-from-computational-models-of-the)
Why do splicing-quantification tools disagree, and which is right when? (sousa-luis-2026-tools-and-tactics-for-studying)


Note: claims above are grounded in the locally ingested sources only. See each
source page for citations.

2026-07 batch additions
holley-1968-alanine-transfer-rna — historical Nobel lecture on sequencing alanine tRNA (the first nucleic-acid sequence; RNA-biology foundation). han-2020-antisense-oligonucleotides-increase-scn1a-expression — TANGO antisense-oligonucleotide upregulation of Scn1a via a poison exon rescues Dravet-model seizures/SUDEP (tango).

wiki/topics/variant-effect.title: Variant Effect & Interpretation
wiki/topics/variant-effect.content:
Variant Effect & Interpretation
Topic hub for variant-effect-prediction — deciding whether, how, and how strongly
a genetic variant alters function, phenotype, or disease risk. It spans deleteriousness
scoring, clinical classification, mechanism inference, and experimental variant assays.
Strands

Scoring & classification. Integrative deleteriousness scores such as CADD
(cadd-score, see kircher-2014-general-framework-for-estimating) and the
ACMG–AMP clinical classification framework (acmg-amp-guidelines, see
richards-2015-standards-and-guidelines-for).
Mechanism. Discriminating gain- vs loss-of-function from gene/protein features
(goflof-database, see sevim-bayrak-2021-identification-discriminative-gene-level)
and structure-based estimation of loss-/gain-of-function and dominant-negative
prevalence across disease (mlof-score, see
badonyi-2025-prevalence-loss-gain-dominant-negative).
Experimental interpretation. Combining computation with multiplexed assays and
deep mutational scanning to resolve variants of uncertain significance (see
spielmann-2022-computational-experimental-methods-classifying-variants).

Sources (5)

kircher-2014-general-framework-for-estimating — CADD relative-pathogenicity framework.
richards-2015-standards-and-guidelines-for — ACMG–AMP variant-interpretation standards.
sevim-bayrak-2021-identification-discriminative-gene-level — gain- vs loss-of-function feature discrimination.
badonyi-2025-prevalence-loss-gain-dominant-negative — mechanism prevalence across genetic disease.
spielmann-2022-computational-experimental-methods-classifying-variants — classifying variants of uncertain significance.

Cross-topic

Adjoins gene-regulation (noncoding-variant effects) and biometrical-genetics
(rare-variant burden); consumes reference/sequencing resources.
